---
title: "Replication Package"
subtitle: "All Code, Data, and Instructions to Reproduce the Analysis"
---

::: {.callout-note}
This page contains everything needed to fully replicate the analysis in the paper. The package follows open science principles: all code is documented, all data is included (subject to licence restrictions), and all figures are reproducible from the provided scripts.
:::

## Package Structure

```
2026_sroi/
├── data/
│   ├── sroi_clean_dataset.csv        # Main dataset: 383 reports × metadata + quality scores
│   ├── sroi_ratios_subset.csv        # Subsample: 64 reports with extractable SROI ratios
│   ├── sroi_factors.csv              # Binary indicators: 11 calculation elements × 383 reports
│   ├── sroi_factors_summary.csv      # Prevalence summary table (11 elements)
│   ├── bootstrap_principle_ci.csv    # Bootstrap CI results (B=10,000)
│   ├── simulation_stats.csv          # Monte Carlo simulation summary statistics
│   └── regression_table.csv          # OLS regression results
│
├── analysis.py                       # Main analysis: quality scoring, descriptive stats, regression
├── simulations.py                    # Bootstrap CIs, permutation tests, Monte Carlo simulation
├── factors_analysis.py               # Calculation element extraction and visualization
├── enrich_dataset.py                 # Metadata enrichment pipeline (sector, country, year)
│
├── figures/                          # All 16 figures (PNG + PDF)
│   ├── fig1_sector_distribution.png
│   ├── fig2_quality_by_principle.png
│   ├── ...
│   └── fig16_quality_ratio_by_factors.png
│
├── draft_v1.qmd                      # Full paper (Quarto)
└── www/                              # This website
```

---

## Data Files

### sroi_clean_dataset.csv

The main dataset. Each row is one SROI report.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `report_id` | str | Unique report identifier |
| `title` | str | Report title |
| `url` | str | Source URL |
| `published_date` | date | Date scraped from website |
| `is_assured` | int | 1 = SVI Report Assurance; 0 = not assured |
| `sector_clean` | str | Sector (15 categories + "other") |
| `country_clean` | str | Country (UK, Australia, USA, ...) |
| `report_type_clean` | str | "Forecast" or "Evaluative" |
| `year_clean` | int | Report year (from PDF text) |
| `org_type` | str | Organisation type |
| `P1` – `P8` | int (0–2) | Quality score for each SVI principle |
| `quality_score` | float | Mean of P1–P8 (0–2 scale) |
| `quality_pct` | float | Quality score as % (0–100) |
| `stakeholder_groups` | int | Number of stakeholder groups identified |

### sroi_ratios_subset.csv

Subsample of 64 reports with extractable SROI ratios.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `report_id` | str | Matches `sroi_clean_dataset.csv` |
| `sroi_ratio` | float | Reported SROI ratio (e.g., 4.44) |
| `investment_value` | float | Total investment (£, where available) |
| All columns from main dataset | — | Merged join |

### sroi_factors.csv

Binary indicators for 11 calculation elements.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `report_id` | str | Unique identifier |
| `deadweight` | int | 1 = deadweight mentioned |
| `attribution` | int | 1 = attribution mentioned |
| `drop_off` | int | 1 = drop-off mentioned |
| `displacement` | int | 1 = displacement mentioned |
| `sensitivity` | int | 1 = sensitivity analysis mentioned |
| `discount_rate` | int | 1 = discount rate mentioned |
| `proxy` | int | 1 = financial proxies mentioned |
| `counterfactual` | int | 1 = counterfactual mentioned |
| `theory_of_change` | int | 1 = theory of change / logic model mentioned |
| `wellby_qaly` | int | 1 = WELLBY / QALY / HACT mentioned |
| `materiality` | int | 1 = materiality discussed |
| `n_factors` | int | Count of adjustment factors (0–4) |

---

## Analysis Code

### analysis.py — Main analysis pipeline

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Main analysis pipeline for the SROI meta-analysis.
Produces: quality scores, descriptive statistics, sector/country summaries,
OLS regression table, and figures 1–5.

Usage: python analysis.py
Outputs: figures/fig1_*.png ... fig5_*.png, data/regression_table.csv
"""
```

**Key functions:**
- `score_principle(text, principle_id)` — keyword scoring for each SVI principle
- `extract_sector(text)` — sector classification from PDF text
- `extract_country(text)` — country identification
- `run_regression(df)` — OLS quality score regression

### simulations.py — Bootstrap, permutation, Monte Carlo

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Statistical simulations:
  1. Bootstrap CIs (B=10,000, BCa method) for all compliance rates
  2. Permutation test for sector-level quality differences
  3. Monte Carlo simulation: implied SROI ratio bias from non-compliance

Usage: python simulations.py
Outputs: figures/fig7_*.png ... fig12_*.png
         data/bootstrap_principle_ci.csv
         data/simulation_stats.csv
"""
```

**Monte Carlo configuration:**

```python
N_SIM = 50_000
SEED = 42

# Beta distributions calibrated to observed ranges
# DW: deadweight | AT: attribution | DO: drop-off | DISP: displacement
CORRECTION_PARAMS = {
    'DW':   (3.5, 10.5),  # Beta(a, b) → mean = a/(a+b) = 0.25
    'AT':   (3.2, 12.8),  # mean = 0.20
    'DO':   (2.0,  8.0),  # mean = 0.20
    'DISP': (1.5, 13.5),  # mean = 0.10
}
```

### factors_analysis.py — Calculation element extraction

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Extracts 11 SROI calculation elements from PDF text using regex patterns.
Computes cascade prevalence, co-occurrence matrix, forecast premium,
and quality-factor relationships.

Usage: python factors_analysis.py
Outputs: figures/fig13_*.png ... fig16_*.png
         data/sroi_factors.csv
         data/sroi_factors_summary.csv
"""

# Keyword patterns (validated against 30 manual reviews)
FACTOR_PATTERNS = {
    'deadweight':    r'deadweight',
    'attribution':   r'attribution',
    'drop_off':      r'drop.?off',
    'displacement':  r'displacement',
    'sensitivity':   r'sensitivity\s+anal',
    'discount_rate': r'discount\s+rate|discount\s+factor',
    'proxy':         r'\bproxy\b|\bproxies\b',
    'counterfactual':r'counterfactual|what would have happened',
    'theory_of_change': r'theory of change|logic model|outcome map',
    'wellby_qaly':   r'wellby|qaly|hact',
    'materiality':   r'materialit',
}
```

---

## How to Run the Full Replication

### Prerequisites

```bash
pip install pandas numpy scipy matplotlib seaborn scikit-learn bibtexparser
```

Or with conda:

```bash
conda install pandas numpy scipy matplotlib seaborn scikit-learn
pip install bibtexparser
```

### Step-by-step

```bash
# 1. Clone / download the replication package
cd Papers/2026_sroi/

# 2. Run metadata enrichment (produces sroi_clean_dataset.csv)
python data/enrich_dataset.py

# 3. Run main analysis (produces figures 1–5 + regression_table.csv)
python data/analysis.py

# 4. Run simulations (produces figures 7–12 + bootstrap/simulation CSVs)
python data/simulations.py

# 5. Run calculation elements analysis (produces figures 13–16 + sroi_factors.csv)
python data/factors_analysis.py

# 6. Render the paper
quarto render draft_v1.qmd

# 7. Render this website
cd www/ && quarto render
```

### Expected runtime

| Script | Expected time |
|--------|--------------|
| `enrich_dataset.py` | ~2–5 minutes (383 PDFs) |
| `analysis.py` | ~30 seconds |
| `simulations.py` | ~2–3 minutes (50,000 MC iterations) |
| `factors_analysis.py` | ~1 minute |

---

## Data Access

The raw SROI reports are publicly available from the Social Value UK reports database at [socialvalueuk.org](https://socialvalueuk.org). The scraped data (`pdf_text_extract` field) contains extracted text from publicly accessible PDFs.

**Licence:** The analysis code is released under MIT licence. The structured dataset (`sroi_clean_dataset.csv`, `sroi_factors.csv`) is released under CC BY 4.0. The raw PDF text extracts are derived from publicly available reports and are provided for research replication only.

---

## Download Files

| File | Description | Size |
|------|-------------|------|
| [sroi_clean_dataset.csv](sroi_clean_dataset.csv) | Main dataset (383 reports) | ~150 KB |
| [sroi_ratios_subset.csv](sroi_ratios_subset.csv) | Ratio subsample (64 reports) | ~25 KB |
| [sroi_factors.csv](sroi_factors.csv) | Calculation elements (383 × 12) | ~45 KB |
| [analysis.py](analysis.py) | Main analysis code | ~8 KB |
| [simulations.py](simulations.py) | Simulation code | ~10 KB |
| [factors_analysis.py](factors_analysis.py) | Factors analysis code | ~12 KB |

---

## Citation

If you use this dataset or code, please cite:

```bibtex
@article{SROIMetaAnalysis2026,
  author    = {[Author]},
  title     = {From Principles to Practice: A Systematic Content Analysis
               of SROI Reporting in the Social Value International Database},
  journal   = {Voluntas: International Journal of Voluntary and Nonprofit Organizations},
  year      = {2026},
  note      = {Forthcoming},
  url       = {https://doi.org/10.1000/placeholder}
}
```
